reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature at Alençon will be higher on a later resolution date. Assuming the unnamed resolution date corresponds to the close date (February 7, 2026) compared to the base date (November 9, 2025): Early November in Alençon typically has a daily mean temperature around 9°C, while early February has a typical daily mean around 4.5°C to 5°C. The difference in means is roughly -4°C to -4.5°C. With the standard deviation of daily average temperatures at a given station around 3.5°C in European maritime winters, the variance of the difference between two largely independent days would yield a joint standard deviation around 4.5°C to 5°C. The probability that February 7 yields a higher average temperature than November 9 can be approximated using a normal distribution for the difference, P(T2 - T1 > 0), giving a z-score of roughly 0.8 to 1.0, which corresponds to a probability of approximately 0.16 to 0.23. I estimate 0.23 to account for slightly higher variance in mid-season extremes and current mild winter trends.
